hero.subtitle: Upload any document. Ask any question. Get precise, cited answers backed by the exact paragraphs that matter.

items.title: Semantic retrieval
items.desc: Vector search finds the most relevant passages across thousands of pages in milliseconds.

items.title: Streaming answers
items.desc: Real-time AI responses with cited sources, so you can trace every claim back to the document.

items.desc: Upload PDFs, Word docs, or plain text — they're indexed and ready to query in seconds.

items.title: Private by default
items.desc: Your documents stay yours. End-to-end encryption, SOC 2 compliant, zero data training.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains what information Docna collects, how it's used, and the choices you have. It applies to everyone who uses the Docna service.

sections.heading: Information we collect
sections.body: Account details you provide (name, email), the documents you upload, and basic usage data (like query counts) needed to operate and improve the service.

sections.heading: How we use your information
sections.body: To provide the core service — indexing your documents and answering your questions — as well as for billing, account support, and service reliability.

sections.heading: Document processing & AI
sections.body: Uploaded documents are processed to generate embeddings and retrieve relevant passages for your questions. Your documents are never used to train shared or third-party models.

sections.heading: Data sharing
sections.body: We don't sell your data. We share the minimum necessary information with service providers — such as our LLM, storage, and payment processors — solely to operate Docna, under contractual confidentiality.

sections.heading: Data retention & deletion
sections.body: Your documents and conversations are retained while your account is active. Deleting a document or your account permanently removes the associated data from our systems.

sections.heading: Security
sections.body: Data is encrypted in transit and at rest. Access to your documents is restricted to your account and to the automated processes that answer your questions.

sections.heading: Your rights
sections.body: You can access, export, or delete your data at any time from your account settings, or by contacting us directly.

sections.heading: Changes to this policy
sections.body: If we make material changes to this policy, we'll notify you by email or through the product before they take effect.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms govern your use of Docna. By creating an account or using the service, you agree to them.

sections.heading: Using Docna
sections.body: You must be able to form a binding contract to use Docna, and you're responsible for the activity that happens under your account.

sections.heading: Subscriptions & billing
sections.body: Paid plans renew automatically for each billing period until cancelled. You can cancel anytime from Billing; you'll retain access until the end of the current period.

sections.heading: Acceptable use
sections.body: Don't upload content you don't have the right to share, and don't use Docna to violate the law or the rights of others, or to abuse or overload the service.

sections.heading: Your content
sections.body: You own the documents you upload. You grant Docna a limited license to process them solely to provide the service to you — nothing more.

sections.heading: AI-generated answers
sections.body: Answers are generated by AI and grounded in your documents, but may still be incomplete or inaccurate. Always verify anything important.

sections.heading: Disclaimers & liability
sections.body: Docna is provided "as is," without warranties of any kind. To the extent permitted by law, Docna isn't liable for indirect or consequential damages arising from your use of the service.

sections.heading: Termination
sections.body: You may stop using Docna and delete your account at any time. We may suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms.

sections.heading: Changes to these terms
sections.body: We may update these terms from time to time; continued use of Docna after a change means you accept the updated terms.
